2215	lastReplayedTLI = XLogRecoveryCtl->lastReplayedTLI;
2216
2217	/*
2218	 * Have we reached the point where our base backup was completed?
2219	 */
2220	if (!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(backupEndPoint) &&
2221		backupEndPoint <= lastReplayedEndRecPtr)
2222	{
2223		XLogRecPtr	saveBackupStartPoint = backupStartPoint;
2224		XLogRecPtr	saveBackupEndPoint = backupEndPoint;
2225
2226		elog(DEBUG1, "end of backup reached");
2227
2228		/*
2229		 * We have reached the end of base backup, as indicated by pg_control.
2230		 * Update the control file accordingly.
2231		 */
2232		ReachedEndOfBackup(lastReplayedEndRecPtr, lastReplayedTLI);
2233		backupStartPoint = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2234		backupEndPoint = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2235		backupEndRequired = false;
2236
2237		ereport(LOG,
2238				(errmsg("completed backup recovery with redo LSN %X/%X and end LSN %X/%X",
2239						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(saveBackupStartPoint),
2240						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(saveBackupEndPoint))));
2241	}
2242
2243	/*
2244	 * Have we passed our safe starting point? Note that minRecoveryPoint is
2245	 * known to be incorrectly set if recovering from a backup, until the
2246	 * XLOG_BACKUP_END arrives to advise us of the correct minRecoveryPoint.
2247	 * All we know prior to that is that we're not consistent yet.
2248	 */
2249	if (!reachedConsistency && !backupEndRequired &&
2250		minRecoveryPoint <= lastReplayedEndRecPtr)
2251	{
2252		/*
2253		 * Check to see if the XLOG sequence contained any unresolved
2254		 * references to uninitialized pages.
2255		 */
2256		XLogCheckInvalidPages();
2257
2258		/*
2259		 * Check that pg_tblspc doesn't contain any real directories. Replay
2260		 * of Database/CREATE_* records may have created fictitious tablespace
2261		 * directories that should have been removed by the time consistency
2262		 * was reached.
2263		 */
2264		CheckTablespaceDirectory();
2265
2266		reachedConsistency = true;
2267		SendPostmasterSignal(PMSIGNAL_RECOVERY_CONSISTENT);
2268		ereport(LOG,
2269				(errmsg("consistent recovery state reached at %X/%X",
2270						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(lastReplayedEndRecPtr))));
2271	}
2272
2273	/*
2274	 * Have we got a valid starting snapshot that will allow queries to be
2275	 * run? If so, we can tell postmaster that the database is consistent now,
2276	 * enabling connections.
2277	 */
2278	if (standbyState == STANDBY_SNAPSHOT_READY &&
2279		!LocalHotStandbyActive &&
2280		reachedConsistency &&
2281		IsUnderPostmaster)
2282	{
2283		SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
2284		XLogRecoveryCtl->SharedHotStandbyActive = true;
2285		SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
2286
2287		LocalHotStandbyActive = true;
2288
2289		SendPostmasterSignal(PMSIGNAL_BEGIN_HOT_STANDBY);
2290	}
2291}
2292
2293/*
2294 * Error context callback for errors occurring during rm_redo().
2295 */
2296static void
2297rm_redo_error_callback(void *arg)
2298{
2299	XLogReaderState *record = (XLogReaderState *) arg;
2300	StringInfoData buf;
2301
2302	initStringInfo(&buf);
2303	xlog_outdesc(&buf, record);
2304	xlog_block_info(&buf, record);
2305
2306	/* translator: %s is a WAL record description */
2307	errcontext("WAL redo at %X/%X for %s",
2308			   LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(record->ReadRecPtr),
2309			   buf.data);
2310
2311	pfree(buf.data);
2312}
2313
2314/*
2315 * Returns a string describing an XLogRecord, consisting of its identity
2316 * optionally followed by a colon, a space, and a further description.
2317 */
2318void
2319xlog_outdesc(StringInfo buf, XLogReaderState *record)
2320{
2321	RmgrData	rmgr = GetRmgr(XLogRecGetRmid(record));
2322	uint8		info = XLogRecGetInfo(record);
2323	const char *id;
2324
2325	appendStringInfoString(buf, rmgr.rm_name);
2326	appendStringInfoChar(buf, '/');
2327
2328	id = rmgr.rm_identify(info);
2329	if (id == NULL)
2330		appendStringInfo(buf, "UNKNOWN (%X): ", info & ~XLR_INFO_MASK);
2331	else
2332		appendStringInfo(buf, "%s: ", id);
2333
2334	rmgr.rm_desc(buf, record);
2335}
2336
2337#ifdef WAL_DEBUG
2338
2339static void
2340xlog_outrec(StringInfo buf, XLogReaderState *record)
2341{
2342	appendStringInfo(buf, "prev %X/%X; xid %u",
2343					 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(XLogRecGetPrev(record)),
2344					 XLogRecGetXid(record));
2345
2346	appendStringInfo(buf, "; len %u",
2347					 XLogRecGetDataLen(record));
2348
2349	xlog_block_info(buf, record);
2350}
2351#endif							/* WAL_DEBUG */
2352
2353/*
2354 * Returns a string giving information about all the blocks in an
2355 * XLogRecord.
2356 */
2357static void
2358xlog_block_info(StringInfo buf, XLogReaderState *record)
2359{
2360	int			block_id;
2361
2362	/* decode block references */
2363	for (block_id = 0; block_id <= XLogRecMaxBlockId(record); block_id++)
2364	{
2365		RelFileLocator rlocator;
2366		ForkNumber	forknum;
2367		BlockNumber blk;
2368
2369		if (!XLogRecGetBlockTagExtended(record, block_id,
2370										&rlocator, &forknum, &blk, NULL))
2371			continue;
2372
2373		if (forknum != MAIN_FORKNUM)
2374			appendStringInfo(buf, "; blkref #%d: rel %u/%u/%u, fork %u, blk %u",
2375							 block_id,
2376							 rlocator.spcOid, rlocator.dbOid,
2377							 rlocator.relNumber,
2378							 forknum,
2379							 blk);
2380		else
2381			appendStringInfo(buf, "; blkref #%d: rel %u/%u/%u, blk %u",
2382							 block_id,
2383							 rlocator.spcOid, rlocator.dbOid,
2384							 rlocator.relNumber,
2385							 blk);
2386		if (XLogRecHasBlockImage(record, block_id))
2387			appendStringInfoString(buf, " FPW");
2388	}
2389}
2390
2391
2392/*
2393 * Check that it's OK to switch to new timeline during recovery.
2394 *
2395 * 'lsn' is the address of the shutdown checkpoint record we're about to
2396 * replay. (Currently, timeline can only change at a shutdown checkpoint).
2397 */
2398static void
2399checkTimeLineSwitch(XLogRecPtr lsn, TimeLineID newTLI, TimeLineID prevTLI,
2400					TimeLineID replayTLI)
2401{
2402	/* Check that the record agrees on what the current (old) timeline is */
2403	if (prevTLI != replayTLI)
2404		ereport(PANIC,
2405				(errmsg("unexpected previous timeline ID %u (current timeline ID %u) in checkpoint record",
2406						prevTLI, replayTLI)));
2407
2408	/*
2409	 * The new timeline better be in the list of timelines we expect to see,
2410	 * according to the timeline history. It should also not decrease.
2411	 */
2412	if (newTLI < replayTLI || !tliInHistory(newTLI, expectedTLEs))
2413		ereport(PANIC,
2414				(errmsg("unexpected timeline ID %u (after %u) in checkpoint record",
2415						newTLI, replayTLI)));
2416
2417	/*
2418	 * If we have not yet reached min recovery point, and we're about to
2419	 * switch to a timeline greater than the timeline of the min recovery
2420	 * point: trouble. After switching to the new timeline, we could not
2421	 * possibly visit the min recovery point on the correct timeline anymore.
2422	 * This can happen if there is a newer timeline in the archive that
2423	 * branched before the timeline the min recovery point is on, and you
2424	 * attempt to do PITR to the new timeline.
2425	 */
2426	if (!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(minRecoveryPoint) &&
2427		lsn < minRecoveryPoint &&
2428		newTLI > minRecoveryPointTLI)
2429		ereport(PANIC,
2430				(errmsg("unexpected timeline ID %u in checkpoint record, before reaching minimum recovery point %X/%X on timeline %u",
2431						newTLI,
2432						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(minRecoveryPoint),
2433						minRecoveryPointTLI)));
2434
2435	/* Looks good */
2436}
2437
2438
2439/*
2440 * Extract timestamp from WAL record.
2441 *
2442 * If the record contains a timestamp, returns true, and saves the timestamp
2443 * in *recordXtime. If the record type has no timestamp, returns false.
2444 * Currently, only transaction commit/abort records and restore points contain
2445 * timestamps.
2446 */
2447static bool
2448getRecordTimestamp(XLogReaderState *record, TimestampTz *recordXtime)
2449{
2450	uint8		info = XLogRecGetInfo(record) & ~XLR_INFO_MASK;
2451	uint8		xact_info = info & XLOG_XACT_OPMASK;
2452	uint8		rmid = XLogRecGetRmid(record);
2453
2454	if (rmid == RM_XLOG_ID && info == XLOG_RESTORE_POINT)
2455	{
2456		*recordXtime = ((xl_restore_point *) XLogRecGetData(record))->rp_time;
2457		return true;
2458	}
2459	if (rmid == RM_XACT_ID && (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT ||
2460							   xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT_PREPARED))
2461	{
2462		*recordXtime = ((xl_xact_commit *) XLogRecGetData(record))->xact_time;
2463		return true;
2464	}
2465	if (rmid == RM_XACT_ID && (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT ||
2466							   xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT_PREPARED))
2467	{
2468		*recordXtime = ((xl_xact_abort *) XLogRecGetData(record))->xact_time;
2469		return true;
2470	}
2471	return false;
2472}
2473
2474/*
2475 * Checks whether the current buffer page and backup page stored in the
2476 * WAL record are consistent or not. Before comparing the two pages, a
2477 * masking can be applied to the pages to ignore certain areas like hint bits,
2478 * unused space between pd_lower and pd_upper among other things. This
2479 * function should be called once WAL replay has been completed for a
2480 * given record.
2481 */
2482static void
2483verifyBackupPageConsistency(XLogReaderState *record)
2484{
2485	RmgrData	rmgr = GetRmgr(XLogRecGetRmid(record));
2486	RelFileLocator rlocator;
2487	ForkNumber	forknum;
2488	BlockNumber blkno;
2489	int			block_id;
2490
2491	/* Records with no backup blocks have no need for consistency checks. */
2492	if (!XLogRecHasAnyBlockRefs(record))
2493		return;
2494
2495	Assert((XLogRecGetInfo(record) & XLR_CHECK_CONSISTENCY) != 0);
2496
2497	for (block_id = 0; block_id <= XLogRecMaxBlockId(record); block_id++)
2498	{
2499		Buffer		buf;
2500		Page		page;
2501
2502		if (!XLogRecGetBlockTagExtended(record, block_id,
2503										&rlocator, &forknum, &blkno, NULL))
2504		{
2505			/*
2506			 * WAL record doesn't contain a block reference with the given id.
2507			 * Do nothing.
2508			 */
2509			continue;
2510		}
2511
2512		Assert(XLogRecHasBlockImage(record, block_id));
2513
2514		if (XLogRecBlockImageApply(record, block_id))
2515		{
2516			/*
2517			 * WAL record has already applied the page, so bypass the
2518			 * consistency check as that would result in comparing the full
2519			 * page stored in the record with itself.
2520			 */
2521			continue;
2522		}
2523
2524		/*
2525		 * Read the contents from the current buffer and store it in a
2526		 * temporary page.
2527		 */
2528		buf = XLogReadBufferExtended(rlocator, forknum, blkno,
2529									 RBM_NORMAL_NO_LOG,
2530									 InvalidBuffer);
2531		if (!BufferIsValid(buf))
2532			continue;
2533
2534		LockBuffer(buf, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
2535		page = BufferGetPage(buf);
2536
2537		/*
2538		 * Take a copy of the local page where WAL has been applied to have a
2539		 * comparison base before masking it...
2540		 */
2541		memcpy(replay_image_masked, page, BLCKSZ);
2542
2543		/* No need for this page anymore now that a copy is in. */
2544		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buf);
2545
2546		/*
2547		 * If the block LSN is already ahead of this WAL record, we can't
2548		 * expect contents to match.  This can happen if recovery is
2549		 * restarted.
2550		 */
2551		if (PageGetLSN(replay_image_masked) > record->EndRecPtr)
2552			continue;
2553
2554		/*
2555		 * Read the contents from the backup copy, stored in WAL record and
2556		 * store it in a temporary page. There is no need to allocate a new
2557		 * page here, a local buffer is fine to hold its contents and a mask
2558		 * can be directly applied on it.
2559		 */
2560		if (!RestoreBlockImage(record, block_id, primary_image_masked))
2561			ereport(ERROR,
2562					(errcode(ERRCODE_INTERNAL_ERROR),
2563					 errmsg_internal("%s", record->errormsg_buf)));
2564
2565		/*
2566		 * If masking function is defined, mask both the primary and replay
2567		 * images
2568		 */
2569		if (rmgr.rm_mask != NULL)
2570		{
2571			rmgr.rm_mask(replay_image_masked, blkno);
2572			rmgr.rm_mask(primary_image_masked, blkno);
2573		}
2574
2575		/* Time to compare the primary and replay images. */
2576		if (memcmp(replay_image_masked, primary_image_masked, BLCKSZ) != 0)
2577		{
2578			elog(FATAL,
2579				 "inconsistent page found, rel %u/%u/%u, forknum %u, blkno %u",
2580				 rlocator.spcOid, rlocator.dbOid, rlocator.relNumber,
2581				 forknum, blkno);
2582		}
2583	}
2584}
2585
2586/*
2587 * For point-in-time recovery, this function decides whether we want to
2588 * stop applying the XLOG before the current record.
2589 *
2590 * Returns true if we are stopping, false otherwise. If stopping, some
2591 * information is saved in recoveryStopXid et al for use in annotating the
2592 * new timeline's history file.
2593 */
2594static bool
2595recoveryStopsBefore(XLogReaderState *record)
2596{
2597	bool		stopsHere = false;
2598	uint8		xact_info;
2599	bool		isCommit;
2600	TimestampTz recordXtime = 0;
2601	TransactionId recordXid;
2602
2603	/*
2604	 * Ignore recovery target settings when not in archive recovery (meaning
2605	 * we are in crash recovery).
2606	 */
2607	if (!ArchiveRecoveryRequested)
2608		return false;
2609
2610	/* Check if we should stop as soon as reaching consistency */
2611	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE && reachedConsistency)
2612	{
2613		ereport(LOG,
2614				(errmsg("recovery stopping after reaching consistency")));
2615
2616		recoveryStopAfter = false;
2617		recoveryStopXid = InvalidTransactionId;
2618		recoveryStopLSN = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2619		recoveryStopTime = 0;
2620		recoveryStopName[0] = '\0';
2621		return true;
2622	}
2623
2624	/* Check if target LSN has been reached */
2625	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_LSN &&
2626		!recoveryTargetInclusive &&
2627		record->ReadRecPtr >= recoveryTargetLSN)
2628	{
2629		recoveryStopAfter = false;
2630		recoveryStopXid = InvalidTransactionId;
2631		recoveryStopLSN = record->ReadRecPtr;
2632		recoveryStopTime = 0;
2633		recoveryStopName[0] = '\0';
2634		ereport(LOG,
2635				(errmsg("recovery stopping before WAL location (LSN) \"%X/%X\"",
2636						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(recoveryStopLSN))));
2637		return true;
2638	}
2639
2640	/* Otherwise we only consider stopping before COMMIT or ABORT records. */
2641	if (XLogRecGetRmid(record) != RM_XACT_ID)
2642		return false;
2643
2644	xact_info = XLogRecGetInfo(record) & XLOG_XACT_OPMASK;
2645
2646	if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT)
2647	{
2648		isCommit = true;
2649		recordXid = XLogRecGetXid(record);
2650	}
2651	else if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT_PREPARED)
2652	{
2653		xl_xact_commit *xlrec = (xl_xact_commit *) XLogRecGetData(record);
2654		xl_xact_parsed_commit parsed;
2655
2656		isCommit = true;
2657		ParseCommitRecord(XLogRecGetInfo(record),
2658						  xlrec,
2659						  &parsed);
2660		recordXid = parsed.twophase_xid;
2661	}
2662	else if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT)
2663	{
2664		isCommit = false;
2665		recordXid = XLogRecGetXid(record);
2666	}
2667	else if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT_PREPARED)
2668	{
2669		xl_xact_abort *xlrec = (xl_xact_abort *) XLogRecGetData(record);
2670		xl_xact_parsed_abort parsed;
2671
2672		isCommit = false;
2673		ParseAbortRecord(XLogRecGetInfo(record),
2674						 xlrec,
2675						 &parsed);
2676		recordXid = parsed.twophase_xid;
2677	}
2678	else
2679		return false;
2680
2681	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_XID && !recoveryTargetInclusive)
2682	{
2683		/*
2684		 * There can be only one transaction end record with this exact
2685		 * transactionid
2686		 *
2687		 * when testing for an xid, we MUST test for equality only, since
2688		 * transactions are numbered in the order they start, not the order
2689		 * they complete. A higher numbered xid will complete before you about
2690		 * 50% of the time...
2691		 */
2692		stopsHere = (recordXid == recoveryTargetXid);
2693	}
2694
2695	/*
2696	 * Note: we must fetch recordXtime regardless of recoveryTarget setting.
2697	 * We don't expect getRecordTimestamp ever to fail, since we already know
2698	 * this is a commit or abort record; but test its result anyway.
2699	 */
2700	if (getRecordTimestamp(record, &recordXtime) &&
2701		recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_TIME)
2702	{
2703		/*
2704		 * There can be many transactions that share the same commit time, so
2705		 * we stop after the last one, if we are inclusive, or stop at the
2706		 * first one if we are exclusive
2707		 */
2708		if (recoveryTargetInclusive)
2709			stopsHere = (recordXtime > recoveryTargetTime);
2710		else
2711			stopsHere = (recordXtime >= recoveryTargetTime);
2712	}
2713
2714	if (stopsHere)
2715	{
2716		recoveryStopAfter = false;
2717		recoveryStopXid = recordXid;
2718		recoveryStopTime = recordXtime;
2719		recoveryStopLSN = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2720		recoveryStopName[0] = '\0';
2721
2722		if (isCommit)
2723		{
2724			ereport(LOG,
2725					(errmsg("recovery stopping before commit of transaction %u, time %s",
2726							recoveryStopXid,
2727							timestamptz_to_str(recoveryStopTime))));
2728		}
2729		else
2730		{
2731			ereport(LOG,
2732					(errmsg("recovery stopping before abort of transaction %u, time %s",
2733							recoveryStopXid,
2734							timestamptz_to_str(recoveryStopTime))));
2735		}
2736	}
2737
2738	return stopsHere;
2739}
2740
2741/*
2742 * Same as recoveryStopsBefore, but called after applying the record.
2743 *
2744 * We also track the timestamp of the latest applied COMMIT/ABORT
2745 * record in XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryLastXTime.
2746 */
2747static bool
2748recoveryStopsAfter(XLogReaderState *record)
2749{
2750	uint8		info;
2751	uint8		xact_info;
2752	uint8		rmid;
2753	TimestampTz recordXtime = 0;
2754
2755	/*
2756	 * Ignore recovery target settings when not in archive recovery (meaning
2757	 * we are in crash recovery).
2758	 */
2759	if (!ArchiveRecoveryRequested)
2760		return false;
2761
2762	info = XLogRecGetInfo(record) & ~XLR_INFO_MASK;
2763	rmid = XLogRecGetRmid(record);
2764
2765	/*
2766	 * There can be many restore points that share the same name; we stop at
2767	 * the first one.
2768	 */
2769	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_NAME &&
2770		rmid == RM_XLOG_ID && info == XLOG_RESTORE_POINT)
2771	{
2772		xl_restore_point *recordRestorePointData;
2773
2774		recordRestorePointData = (xl_restore_point *) XLogRecGetData(record);
2775
2776		if (strcmp(recordRestorePointData->rp_name, recoveryTargetName) == 0)
2777		{
2778			recoveryStopAfter = true;
2779			recoveryStopXid = InvalidTransactionId;
2780			recoveryStopLSN = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2781			(void) getRecordTimestamp(record, &recoveryStopTime);
2782			strlcpy(recoveryStopName, recordRestorePointData->rp_name, MAXFNAMELEN);
2783
2784			ereport(LOG,
2785					(errmsg("recovery stopping at restore point \"%s\", time %s",
2786							recoveryStopName,
2787							timestamptz_to_str(recoveryStopTime))));
2788			return true;
2789		}
2790	}
2791
2792	/* Check if the target LSN has been reached */
2793	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_LSN &&
2794		recoveryTargetInclusive &&
2795		record->ReadRecPtr >= recoveryTargetLSN)
2796	{
2797		recoveryStopAfter = true;
2798		recoveryStopXid = InvalidTransactionId;
2799		recoveryStopLSN = record->ReadRecPtr;
2800		recoveryStopTime = 0;
2801		recoveryStopName[0] = '\0';
2802		ereport(LOG,
2803				(errmsg("recovery stopping after WAL location (LSN) \"%X/%X\"",
2804						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(recoveryStopLSN))));
2805		return true;
2806	}
2807
2808	if (rmid != RM_XACT_ID)
2809		return false;
2810
2811	xact_info = info & XLOG_XACT_OPMASK;
2812
2813	if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT ||
2814		xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT_PREPARED ||
2815		xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT ||
2816		xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT_PREPARED)
2817	{
2818		TransactionId recordXid;
2819
2820		/* Update the last applied transaction timestamp */
2821		if (getRecordTimestamp(record, &recordXtime))
2822			SetLatestXTime(recordXtime);
2823
2824		/* Extract the XID of the committed/aborted transaction */
2825		if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT_PREPARED)
2826		{
2827			xl_xact_commit *xlrec = (xl_xact_commit *) XLogRecGetData(record);
2828			xl_xact_parsed_commit parsed;
2829
2830			ParseCommitRecord(XLogRecGetInfo(record),
2831							  xlrec,
2832							  &parsed);
2833			recordXid = parsed.twophase_xid;
2834		}
2835		else if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT_PREPARED)
2836		{
2837			xl_xact_abort *xlrec = (xl_xact_abort *) XLogRecGetData(record);
2838			xl_xact_parsed_abort parsed;
2839
2840			ParseAbortRecord(XLogRecGetInfo(record),
2841							 xlrec,
2842							 &parsed);
2843			recordXid = parsed.twophase_xid;
2844		}
2845		else
2846			recordXid = XLogRecGetXid(record);
2847
2848		/*
2849		 * There can be only one transaction end record with this exact
2850		 * transactionid
2851		 *
2852		 * when testing for an xid, we MUST test for equality only, since
2853		 * transactions are numbered in the order they start, not the order
2854		 * they complete. A higher numbered xid will complete before you about
2855		 * 50% of the time...
2856		 */
2857		if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_XID && recoveryTargetInclusive &&
2858			recordXid == recoveryTargetXid)
2859		{
2860			recoveryStopAfter = true;
2861			recoveryStopXid = recordXid;
2862			recoveryStopTime = recordXtime;
2863			recoveryStopLSN = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2864			recoveryStopName[0] = '\0';
2865
2866			if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT ||
2867				xact_info == XLOG_XACT_COMMIT_PREPARED)
2868			{
2869				ereport(LOG,
2870						(errmsg("recovery stopping after commit of transaction %u, time %s",
2871								recoveryStopXid,
2872								timestamptz_to_str(recoveryStopTime))));
2873			}
2874			else if (xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT ||
2875					 xact_info == XLOG_XACT_ABORT_PREPARED)
2876			{
2877				ereport(LOG,
2878						(errmsg("recovery stopping after abort of transaction %u, time %s",
2879								recoveryStopXid,
2880								timestamptz_to_str(recoveryStopTime))));
2881			}
2882			return true;
2883		}
2884	}
2885
2886	/* Check if we should stop as soon as reaching consistency */
2887	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE && reachedConsistency)
2888	{
2889		ereport(LOG,
2890				(errmsg("recovery stopping after reaching consistency")));
2891
2892		recoveryStopAfter = true;
2893		recoveryStopXid = InvalidTransactionId;
2894		recoveryStopTime = 0;
2895		recoveryStopLSN = InvalidXLogRecPtr;
2896		recoveryStopName[0] = '\0';
2897		return true;
2898	}
2899
2900	return false;
2901}
2902
2903/*
2904 * Create a comment for the history file to explain why and where
2905 * timeline changed.
2906 */
2907static char *
2908getRecoveryStopReason(void)
2909{
2910	char		reason[200];
2911
2912	if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_XID)
2913		snprintf(reason, sizeof(reason),
2914				 "%s transaction %u",
2915				 recoveryStopAfter ? "after" : "before",
2916				 recoveryStopXid);
2917	else if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_TIME)
2918		snprintf(reason, sizeof(reason),
2919				 "%s %s\n",
2920				 recoveryStopAfter ? "after" : "before",
2921				 timestamptz_to_str(recoveryStopTime));
2922	else if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_LSN)
2923		snprintf(reason, sizeof(reason),
2924				 "%s LSN %X/%X\n",
2925				 recoveryStopAfter ? "after" : "before",
2926				 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(recoveryStopLSN));
2927	else if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_NAME)
2928		snprintf(reason, sizeof(reason),
2929				 "at restore point \"%s\"",
2930				 recoveryStopName);
2931	else if (recoveryTarget == RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE)
2932		snprintf(reason, sizeof(reason), "reached consistency");
2933	else
2934		snprintf(reason, sizeof(reason), "no recovery target specified");
2935
2936	return pstrdup(reason);
2937}
2938
2939/*
2940 * Wait until shared recoveryPauseState is set to RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED.
2941 *
2942 * endOfRecovery is true if the recovery target is reached and
2943 * the paused state starts at the end of recovery because of
2944 * recovery_target_action=pause, and false otherwise.
2945 */
2946static void
2947recoveryPausesHere(bool endOfRecovery)
2948{
2949	/* Don't pause unless users can connect! */
2950	if (!LocalHotStandbyActive)
2951		return;
2952
2953	/* Don't pause after standby promotion has been triggered */
2954	if (LocalPromoteIsTriggered)
2955		return;
2956
2957	if (endOfRecovery)
2958		ereport(LOG,
2959				(errmsg("pausing at the end of recovery"),
2960				 errhint("Execute pg_wal_replay_resume() to promote.")));
2961	else
2962		ereport(LOG,
2963				(errmsg("recovery has paused"),
2964				 errhint("Execute pg_wal_replay_resume() to continue.")));
2965
2966	/* loop until recoveryPauseState is set to RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED */
2967	while (GetRecoveryPauseState() != RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED)
2968	{
2969		ProcessStartupProcInterrupts();
2970		if (CheckForStandbyTrigger())
2971			return;
2972
2973		/*
2974		 * If recovery pause is requested then set it paused.  While we are in
2975		 * the loop, user might resume and pause again so set this every time.
2976		 */
2977		ConfirmRecoveryPaused();
2978
2979		/*
2980		 * We wait on a condition variable that will wake us as soon as the
2981		 * pause ends, but we use a timeout so we can check the above exit
2982		 * condition periodically too.
2983		 */
2984		ConditionVariableTimedSleep(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryNotPausedCV, 1000,
2985									WAIT_EVENT_RECOVERY_PAUSE);
2986	}
2987	ConditionVariableCancelSleep();
2988}
2989
2990/*
2991 * When recovery_min_apply_delay is set, we wait long enough to make sure
2992 * certain record types are applied at least that interval behind the primary.
2993 *
2994 * Returns true if we waited.
2995 *
2996 * Note that the delay is calculated between the WAL record log time and
2997 * the current time on standby. We would prefer to keep track of when this
2998 * standby received each WAL record, which would allow a more consistent
2999 * approach and one not affected by time synchronisation issues, but that
3000 * is significantly more effort and complexity for little actual gain in
3001 * usability.
3002 */
3003static bool
3004recoveryApplyDelay(XLogReaderState *record)
3005{
3006	uint8		xact_info;
3007	TimestampTz xtime;
3008	TimestampTz delayUntil;
3009	long		msecs;
3010
3011	/* nothing to do if no delay configured */
3012	if (recovery_min_apply_delay <= 0)
3013		return false;
3014
3015	/* no delay is applied on a database not yet consistent */
3016	if (!reachedConsistency)
3017		return false;
3018
3019	/* nothing to do if crash recovery is requested */
3020	if (!ArchiveRecoveryRequested)
3021		return false;
3022
3023	/*
3024	 * Is it a COMMIT record?
3025	 *
3026	 * We deliberately choose not to delay aborts since they have no effect on
3027	 * MVCC. We already allow replay of records that don't have a timestamp,
3028	 * so there is already opportunity for issues caused by early conflicts on
3029	 * standbys.
3030	 */
3031	if (XLogRecGetRmid(record) != RM_XACT_ID)
3032		return false;
3033
3034	xact_info = XLogRecGetInfo(record) & XLOG_XACT_OPMASK;
3035
3036	if (xact_info != XLOG_XACT_COMMIT &&
3037		xact_info != XLOG_XACT_COMMIT_PREPARED)
3038		return false;
3039
3040	if (!getRecordTimestamp(record, &xtime))
3041		return false;
3042
3043	delayUntil = TimestampTzPlusMilliseconds(xtime, recovery_min_apply_delay);
3044
3045	/*
3046	 * Exit without arming the latch if it's already past time to apply this
3047	 * record
3048	 */
3049	msecs = TimestampDifferenceMilliseconds(GetCurrentTimestamp(), delayUntil);
3050	if (msecs <= 0)
3051		return false;
3052
3053	while (true)
3054	{
3055		ResetLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch);
3056
3057		/* This might change recovery_min_apply_delay. */
3058		ProcessStartupProcInterrupts();
3059
3060		if (CheckForStandbyTrigger())
3061			break;
3062
3063		/*
3064		 * Recalculate delayUntil as recovery_min_apply_delay could have
3065		 * changed while waiting in this loop.
3066		 */
3067		delayUntil = TimestampTzPlusMilliseconds(xtime, recovery_min_apply_delay);
3068
3069		/*
3070		 * Wait for difference between GetCurrentTimestamp() and delayUntil.
3071		 */
3072		msecs = TimestampDifferenceMilliseconds(GetCurrentTimestamp(),
3073												delayUntil);
3074
3075		if (msecs <= 0)
3076			break;
3077
3078		elog(DEBUG2, "recovery apply delay %ld milliseconds", msecs);
3079
3080		(void) WaitLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch,
3081						 WL_LATCH_SET | WL_TIMEOUT | WL_EXIT_ON_PM_DEATH,
3082						 msecs,
3083						 WAIT_EVENT_RECOVERY_APPLY_DELAY);
3084	}
3085	return true;
3086}
3087
3088/*
3089 * Get the current state of the recovery pause request.
3090 */
3091RecoveryPauseState
3092GetRecoveryPauseState(void)
3093{
3094	RecoveryPauseState state;
3095
3096	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
3097	state = XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryPauseState;
3098	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
3099
3100	return state;
3101}
3102
3103/*
3104 * Set the recovery pause state.
3105 *
3106 * If recovery pause is requested then sets the recovery pause state to
3107 * 'pause requested' if it is not already 'paused'.  Otherwise, sets it
3108 * to 'not paused' to resume the recovery.  The recovery pause will be
3109 * confirmed by the ConfirmRecoveryPaused.
3110 */
3111void
3112SetRecoveryPause(bool recoveryPause)
3113{
3114	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
3115
3116	if (!recoveryPause)
3117		XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryPauseState = RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED;
3118	else if (XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryPauseState == RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED)
3119		XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryPauseState = RECOVERY_PAUSE_REQUESTED;
3120
3121	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
3122
3123	if (!recoveryPause)
3124		ConditionVariableBroadcast(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryNotPausedCV);
3125}
3126
3127/*
3128 * Confirm the recovery pause by setting the recovery pause state to
3129 * RECOVERY_PAUSED.
3130 */
3131static void
3132ConfirmRecoveryPaused(void)
3133{
3134	/* If recovery pause is requested then set it paused */
3135	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
3136	if (XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryPauseState == RECOVERY_PAUSE_REQUESTED)
3137		XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryPauseState = RECOVERY_PAUSED;
3138	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
3139}
3140
3141
3142/*
3143 * Attempt to read the next XLOG record.
3144 *
3145 * Before first call, the reader needs to be positioned to the first record
3146 * by calling XLogPrefetcherBeginRead().
3147 *
3148 * If no valid record is available, returns NULL, or fails if emode is PANIC.
3149 * (emode must be either PANIC, LOG). In standby mode, retries until a valid
3150 * record is available.
3151 */
3152static XLogRecord *
3153ReadRecord(XLogPrefetcher *xlogprefetcher, int emode,
3154		   bool fetching_ckpt, TimeLineID replayTLI)
3155{
3156	XLogRecord *record;
3157	XLogReaderState *xlogreader = XLogPrefetcherGetReader(xlogprefetcher);
3158	XLogPageReadPrivate *private = (XLogPageReadPrivate *) xlogreader->private_data;
3159
3160	/* Pass through parameters to XLogPageRead */
3161	private->fetching_ckpt = fetching_ckpt;
3162	private->emode = emode;
3163	private->randAccess = (xlogreader->ReadRecPtr == InvalidXLogRecPtr);
3164	private->replayTLI = replayTLI;
3165
3166	/* This is the first attempt to read this page. */
3167	lastSourceFailed = false;
3168
3169	for (;;)
3170	{
3171		char	   *errormsg;
3172
3173		record = XLogPrefetcherReadRecord(xlogprefetcher, &errormsg);
3174		if (record == NULL)
3175		{
3176			/*
3177			 * When we find that WAL ends in an incomplete record, keep track
3178			 * of that record.  After recovery is done, we'll write a record
3179			 * to indicate to downstream WAL readers that that portion is to
3180			 * be ignored.
3181			 *
3182			 * However, when ArchiveRecoveryRequested = true, we're going to
3183			 * switch to a new timeline at the end of recovery. We will only
3184			 * copy WAL over to the new timeline up to the end of the last
3185			 * complete record, so if we did this, we would later create an
3186			 * overwrite contrecord in the wrong place, breaking everything.
3187			 */
3188			if (!ArchiveRecoveryRequested &&
3189				!XLogRecPtrIsInvalid(xlogreader->abortedRecPtr))
3190			{
3191				abortedRecPtr = xlogreader->abortedRecPtr;
3192				missingContrecPtr = xlogreader->missingContrecPtr;
3193			}
3194
3195			if (readFile >= 0)
3196			{
3197				close(readFile);
3198				readFile = -1;
3199			}
3200
3201			/*
3202			 * We only end up here without a message when XLogPageRead()
3203			 * failed - in that case we already logged something. In
3204			 * StandbyMode that only happens if we have been triggered, so we
3205			 * shouldn't loop anymore in that case.
3206			 */
3207			if (errormsg)
3208				ereport(emode_for_corrupt_record(emode, xlogreader->EndRecPtr),
3209						(errmsg_internal("%s", errormsg) /* already translated */ ));
3210		}
3211
3212		/*
3213		 * Check page TLI is one of the expected values.
3214		 */
3215		else if (!tliInHistory(xlogreader->latestPageTLI, expectedTLEs))
3216		{
3217			char		fname[MAXFNAMELEN];
3218			XLogSegNo	segno;
3219			int32		offset;
3220
3221			XLByteToSeg(xlogreader->latestPagePtr, segno, wal_segment_size);
3222			offset = XLogSegmentOffset(xlogreader->latestPagePtr,
3223									   wal_segment_size);
3224			XLogFileName(fname, xlogreader->seg.ws_tli, segno,
3225						 wal_segment_size);
3226			ereport(emode_for_corrupt_record(emode, xlogreader->EndRecPtr),
3227					(errmsg("unexpected timeline ID %u in WAL segment %s, LSN %X/%X, offset %u",
3228							xlogreader->latestPageTLI,
3229							fname,
3230							LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(xlogreader->latestPagePtr),
3231							offset)));
3232			record = NULL;
3233		}
3234
3235		if (record)
3236		{
3237			/* Great, got a record */
3238			return record;
3239		}
3240		else
3241		{
3242			/* No valid record available from this source */
3243			lastSourceFailed = true;
3244
3245			/*
3246			 * If archive recovery was requested, but we were still doing
3247			 * crash recovery, switch to archive recovery and retry using the
3248			 * offline archive. We have now replayed all the valid WAL in
3249			 * pg_wal, so we are presumably now consistent.
3250			 *
3251			 * We require that there's at least some valid WAL present in
3252			 * pg_wal, however (!fetching_ckpt).  We could recover using the
3253			 * WAL from the archive, even if pg_wal is completely empty, but
3254			 * we'd have no idea how far we'd have to replay to reach
3255			 * consistency.  So err on the safe side and give up.
3256			 */
3257			if (!InArchiveRecovery && ArchiveRecoveryRequested &&
3258				!fetching_ckpt)
3259			{
3260				ereport(DEBUG1,
3261						(errmsg_internal("reached end of WAL in pg_wal, entering archive recovery")));
3262				InArchiveRecovery = true;
3263				if (StandbyModeRequested)
3264					EnableStandbyMode();
3265
3266				SwitchIntoArchiveRecovery(xlogreader->EndRecPtr, replayTLI);
3267				minRecoveryPoint = xlogreader->EndRecPtr;
3268				minRecoveryPointTLI = replayTLI;
3269
3270				CheckRecoveryConsistency();
3271
3272				/*
3273				 * Before we retry, reset lastSourceFailed and currentSource
3274				 * so that we will check the archive next.
3275				 */
3276				lastSourceFailed = false;
3277				currentSource = XLOG_FROM_ANY;
3278
3279				continue;
3280			}
3281
3282			/* In standby mode, loop back to retry. Otherwise, give up. */
3283			if (StandbyMode && !CheckForStandbyTrigger())
3284				continue;
3285			else
3286				return NULL;
3287		}
3288	}
3289}
3290
3291/*
3292 * Read the XLOG page containing targetPagePtr into readBuf (if not read
3293 * already).  Returns number of bytes read, if the page is read successfully,
3294 * or XLREAD_FAIL in case of errors.  When errors occur, they are ereport'ed,
3295 * but only if they have not been previously reported.
3296 *
3297 * See XLogReaderRoutine.page_read for more details.
3298 *
3299 * While prefetching, xlogreader->nonblocking may be set.  In that case,
3300 * returns XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK if we'd otherwise have to wait for more WAL.
3301 *
3302 * This is responsible for restoring files from archive as needed, as well
3303 * as for waiting for the requested WAL record to arrive in standby mode.
3304 *
3305 * xlogreader->private_data->emode specifies the log level used for reporting
3306 * "file not found" or "end of WAL" situations in archive recovery, or in
3307 * standby mode when promotion is triggered. If set to WARNING or below,
3308 * XLogPageRead() returns XLREAD_FAIL in those situations, on higher log
3309 * levels the ereport() won't return.
3310 *
3311 * In standby mode, if after a successful return of XLogPageRead() the
3312 * caller finds the record it's interested in to be broken, it should
3313 * ereport the error with the level determined by
3314 * emode_for_corrupt_record(), and then set lastSourceFailed
3315 * and call XLogPageRead() again with the same arguments. This lets
3316 * XLogPageRead() to try fetching the record from another source, or to
3317 * sleep and retry.
3318 */
3319static int
3320XLogPageRead(XLogReaderState *xlogreader, XLogRecPtr targetPagePtr, int reqLen,
3321			 XLogRecPtr targetRecPtr, char *readBuf)
3322{
3323	XLogPageReadPrivate *private =
3324		(XLogPageReadPrivate *) xlogreader->private_data;
3325	int			emode = private->emode;
3326	uint32		targetPageOff;
3327	XLogSegNo	targetSegNo PG_USED_FOR_ASSERTS_ONLY;
3328	int			r;
3329	instr_time	io_start;
3330
3331	XLByteToSeg(targetPagePtr, targetSegNo, wal_segment_size);
3332	targetPageOff = XLogSegmentOffset(targetPagePtr, wal_segment_size);
3333
3334	/*
3335	 * See if we need to switch to a new segment because the requested record
3336	 * is not in the currently open one.
3337	 */
3338	if (readFile >= 0 &&
3339		!XLByteInSeg(targetPagePtr, readSegNo, wal_segment_size))
3340	{
3341		/*
3342		 * Request a restartpoint if we've replayed too much xlog since the
3343		 * last one.
3344		 */
3345		if (ArchiveRecoveryRequested && IsUnderPostmaster)
3346		{
3347			if (XLogCheckpointNeeded(readSegNo))
3348			{
3349				(void) GetRedoRecPtr();
3350				if (XLogCheckpointNeeded(readSegNo))
3351					RequestCheckpoint(CHECKPOINT_CAUSE_XLOG);
3352			}
3353		}
3354
3355		close(readFile);
3356		readFile = -1;
3357		readSource = XLOG_FROM_ANY;
3358	}
3359
3360	XLByteToSeg(targetPagePtr, readSegNo, wal_segment_size);
3361
3362retry:
3363	/* See if we need to retrieve more data */
3364	if (readFile < 0 ||
3365		(readSource == XLOG_FROM_STREAM &&
3366		 flushedUpto < targetPagePtr + reqLen))
3367	{
3368		if (readFile >= 0 &&
3369			xlogreader->nonblocking &&
3370			readSource == XLOG_FROM_STREAM &&
3371			flushedUpto < targetPagePtr + reqLen)
3372			return XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK;
3373
3374		switch (WaitForWALToBecomeAvailable(targetPagePtr + reqLen,
3375											private->randAccess,
3376											private->fetching_ckpt,
3377											targetRecPtr,
3378											private->replayTLI,
3379											xlogreader->EndRecPtr,
3380											xlogreader->nonblocking))
3381		{
3382			case XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK:
3383				return XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK;
3384			case XLREAD_FAIL:
3385				if (readFile >= 0)
3386					close(readFile);
3387				readFile = -1;
3388				readLen = 0;
3389				readSource = XLOG_FROM_ANY;
3390				return XLREAD_FAIL;
3391			case XLREAD_SUCCESS:
3392				break;
3393		}
3394	}
3395
3396	/*
3397	 * At this point, we have the right segment open and if we're streaming we
3398	 * know the requested record is in it.
3399	 */
3400	Assert(readFile != -1);
3401
3402	/*
3403	 * If the current segment is being streamed from the primary, calculate
3404	 * how much of the current page we have received already. We know the
3405	 * requested record has been received, but this is for the benefit of
3406	 * future calls, to allow quick exit at the top of this function.
3407	 */
3408	if (readSource == XLOG_FROM_STREAM)
3409	{
3410		if (((targetPagePtr) / XLOG_BLCKSZ) != (flushedUpto / XLOG_BLCKSZ))
3411			readLen = XLOG_BLCKSZ;
3412		else
3413			readLen = XLogSegmentOffset(flushedUpto, wal_segment_size) -
3414				targetPageOff;
3415	}
3416	else
3417		readLen = XLOG_BLCKSZ;
3418
3419	/* Read the requested page */
3420	readOff = targetPageOff;
3421
3422	/* Measure I/O timing when reading segment */
3423	io_start = pgstat_prepare_io_time(track_wal_io_timing);
3424
3425	pgstat_report_wait_start(WAIT_EVENT_WAL_READ);
3426	r = pg_pread(readFile, readBuf, XLOG_BLCKSZ, (off_t) readOff);
3427	if (r != XLOG_BLCKSZ)
3428	{
3429		char		fname[MAXFNAMELEN];
3430		int			save_errno = errno;
3431
3432		pgstat_report_wait_end();
3433
3434		pgstat_count_io_op_time(IOOBJECT_WAL, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL, IOOP_READ,
3435								io_start, 1, r);
3436
3437		XLogFileName(fname, curFileTLI, readSegNo, wal_segment_size);
3438		if (r < 0)
3439		{
3440			errno = save_errno;
3441			ereport(emode_for_corrupt_record(emode, targetPagePtr + reqLen),
3442					(errcode_for_file_access(),
3443					 errmsg("could not read from WAL segment %s, LSN %X/%X, offset %u: %m",
3444							fname, LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(targetPagePtr),
3445							readOff)));
3446		}
3447		else
3448			ereport(emode_for_corrupt_record(emode, targetPagePtr + reqLen),
3449					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATA_CORRUPTED),
3450					 errmsg("could not read from WAL segment %s, LSN %X/%X, offset %u: read %d of %zu",
3451							fname, LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(targetPagePtr),
3452							readOff, r, (Size) XLOG_BLCKSZ)));
3453		goto next_record_is_invalid;
3454	}
3455	pgstat_report_wait_end();
3456
3457	pgstat_count_io_op_time(IOOBJECT_WAL, IOCONTEXT_NORMAL, IOOP_READ,
3458							io_start, 1, r);
3459
3460	Assert(targetSegNo == readSegNo);
3461	Assert(targetPageOff == readOff);
3462	Assert(reqLen <= readLen);
3463
3464	xlogreader->seg.ws_tli = curFileTLI;
3465
3466	/*
3467	 * Check the page header immediately, so that we can retry immediately if
3468	 * it's not valid. This may seem unnecessary, because ReadPageInternal()
3469	 * validates the page header anyway, and would propagate the failure up to
3470	 * ReadRecord(), which would retry. However, there's a corner case with
3471	 * continuation records, if a record is split across two pages such that
3472	 * we would need to read the two pages from different sources across two
3473	 * WAL segments.
3474	 *
3475	 * The first page is only available locally, in pg_wal, because it's
3476	 * already been recycled on the primary. The second page, however, is not
3477	 * present in pg_wal, and we should stream it from the primary. There is a
3478	 * recycled WAL segment present in pg_wal, with garbage contents, however.
3479	 * We would read the first page from the local WAL segment, but when
3480	 * reading the second page, we would read the bogus, recycled, WAL
3481	 * segment. If we didn't catch that case here, we would never recover,
3482	 * because ReadRecord() would retry reading the whole record from the
3483	 * beginning.
3484	 *
3485	 * Of course, this only catches errors in the page header, which is what
3486	 * happens in the case of a recycled WAL segment. Other kinds of errors or
3487	 * corruption still has the same problem. But this at least fixes the
3488	 * common case, which can happen as part of normal operation.
3489	 *
3490	 * Validating the page header is cheap enough that doing it twice
3491	 * shouldn't be a big deal from a performance point of view.
3492	 *
3493	 * When not in standby mode, an invalid page header should cause recovery
3494	 * to end, not retry reading the page, so we don't need to validate the
3495	 * page header here for the retry. Instead, ReadPageInternal() is
3496	 * responsible for the validation.
3497	 */
3498	if (StandbyMode &&
3499		(targetPagePtr % wal_segment_size) == 0 &&
3500		!XLogReaderValidatePageHeader(xlogreader, targetPagePtr, readBuf))
3501	{
3502		/*
3503		 * Emit this error right now then retry this page immediately. Use
3504		 * errmsg_internal() because the message was already translated.
3505		 */
3506		if (xlogreader->errormsg_buf[0])
3507			ereport(emode_for_corrupt_record(emode, xlogreader->EndRecPtr),
3508					(errmsg_internal("%s", xlogreader->errormsg_buf)));
3509
3510		/* reset any error XLogReaderValidatePageHeader() might have set */
3511		XLogReaderResetError(xlogreader);
3512		goto next_record_is_invalid;
3513	}
3514
3515	return readLen;
3516
3517next_record_is_invalid:
3518
3519	/*
3520	 * If we're reading ahead, give up fast.  Retries and error reporting will
3521	 * be handled by a later read when recovery catches up to this point.
3522	 */
3523	if (xlogreader->nonblocking)
3524		return XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK;
3525
3526	lastSourceFailed = true;
3527
3528	if (readFile >= 0)
3529		close(readFile);
3530	readFile = -1;
3531	readLen = 0;
3532	readSource = XLOG_FROM_ANY;
3533
3534	/* In standby-mode, keep trying */
3535	if (StandbyMode)
3536		goto retry;
3537	else
3538		return XLREAD_FAIL;
3539}
3540
3541/*
3542 * Open the WAL segment containing WAL location 'RecPtr'.
3543 *
3544 * The segment can be fetched via restore_command, or via walreceiver having
3545 * streamed the record, or it can already be present in pg_wal. Checking
3546 * pg_wal is mainly for crash recovery, but it will be polled in standby mode
3547 * too, in case someone copies a new segment directly to pg_wal. That is not
3548 * documented or recommended, though.
3549 *
3550 * If 'fetching_ckpt' is true, we're fetching a checkpoint record, and should
3551 * prepare to read WAL starting from RedoStartLSN after this.
3552 *
3553 * 'RecPtr' might not point to the beginning of the record we're interested
3554 * in, it might also point to the page or segment header. In that case,
3555 * 'tliRecPtr' is the position of the WAL record we're interested in. It is
3556 * used to decide which timeline to stream the requested WAL from.
3557 *
3558 * 'replayLSN' is the current replay LSN, so that if we scan for new
3559 * timelines, we can reject a switch to a timeline that branched off before
3560 * this point.
3561 *
3562 * If the record is not immediately available, the function returns false
3563 * if we're not in standby mode. In standby mode, waits for it to become
3564 * available.
3565 *
3566 * When the requested record becomes available, the function opens the file
3567 * containing it (if not open already), and returns XLREAD_SUCCESS. When end
3568 * of standby mode is triggered by the user, and there is no more WAL
3569 * available, returns XLREAD_FAIL.
3570 *
3571 * If nonblocking is true, then give up immediately if we can't satisfy the
3572 * request, returning XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK instead of waiting.
3573 */
3574static XLogPageReadResult
3575WaitForWALToBecomeAvailable(XLogRecPtr RecPtr, bool randAccess,
3576							bool fetching_ckpt, XLogRecPtr tliRecPtr,
3577							TimeLineID replayTLI, XLogRecPtr replayLSN,
3578							bool nonblocking)
3579{
3580	static TimestampTz last_fail_time = 0;
3581	TimestampTz now;
3582	bool		streaming_reply_sent = false;
3583
3584	/*-------
3585	 * Standby mode is implemented by a state machine:
3586	 *
3587	 * 1. Read from either archive or pg_wal (XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE), or just
3588	 *	  pg_wal (XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL)
3589	 * 2. Check for promotion trigger request
3590	 * 3. Read from primary server via walreceiver (XLOG_FROM_STREAM)
3591	 * 4. Rescan timelines
3592	 * 5. Sleep wal_retrieve_retry_interval milliseconds, and loop back to 1.
3593	 *
3594	 * Failure to read from the current source advances the state machine to
3595	 * the next state.
3596	 *
3597	 * 'currentSource' indicates the current state. There are no currentSource
3598	 * values for "check trigger", "rescan timelines", and "sleep" states,
3599	 * those actions are taken when reading from the previous source fails, as
3600	 * part of advancing to the next state.
3601	 *
3602	 * If standby mode is turned off while reading WAL from stream, we move
3603	 * to XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE and reset lastSourceFailed, to force fetching
3604	 * the files (which would be required at end of recovery, e.g., timeline
3605	 * history file) from archive or pg_wal. We don't need to kill WAL receiver
3606	 * here because it's already stopped when standby mode is turned off at
3607	 * the end of recovery.
3608	 *-------
3609	 */
3610	if (!InArchiveRecovery)
3611		currentSource = XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL;
3612	else if (currentSource == XLOG_FROM_ANY ||
3613			 (!StandbyMode && currentSource == XLOG_FROM_STREAM))
3614	{
3615		lastSourceFailed = false;
3616		currentSource = XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE;
3617	}
3618
3619	for (;;)
3620	{
3621		XLogSource	oldSource = currentSource;
3622		bool		startWalReceiver = false;
3623
3624		/*
3625		 * First check if we failed to read from the current source, and
3626		 * advance the state machine if so. The failure to read might've
3627		 * happened outside this function, e.g when a CRC check fails on a
3628		 * record, or within this loop.
3629		 */
3630		if (lastSourceFailed)
3631		{
3632			/*
3633			 * Don't allow any retry loops to occur during nonblocking
3634			 * readahead.  Let the caller process everything that has been
3635			 * decoded already first.
3636			 */
3637			if (nonblocking)
3638				return XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK;
3639
3640			switch (currentSource)
3641			{
3642				case XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE:
3643				case XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL:
3644
3645					/*
3646					 * Check to see if promotion is requested. Note that we do
3647					 * this only after failure, so when you promote, we still
3648					 * finish replaying as much as we can from archive and
3649					 * pg_wal before failover.
3650					 */
3651					if (StandbyMode && CheckForStandbyTrigger())
3652					{
3653						XLogShutdownWalRcv();
3654						return XLREAD_FAIL;
3655					}
3656
3657					/*
3658					 * Not in standby mode, and we've now tried the archive
3659					 * and pg_wal.
3660					 */
3661					if (!StandbyMode)
3662						return XLREAD_FAIL;
3663
3664					/*
3665					 * Move to XLOG_FROM_STREAM state, and set to start a
3666					 * walreceiver if necessary.
3667					 */
3668					currentSource = XLOG_FROM_STREAM;
3669					startWalReceiver = true;
3670					break;
3671
3672				case XLOG_FROM_STREAM:
3673
3674					/*
3675					 * Failure while streaming. Most likely, we got here
3676					 * because streaming replication was terminated, or
3677					 * promotion was triggered. But we also get here if we
3678					 * find an invalid record in the WAL streamed from the
3679					 * primary, in which case something is seriously wrong.
3680					 * There's little chance that the problem will just go
3681					 * away, but PANIC is not good for availability either,
3682					 * especially in hot standby mode. So, we treat that the
3683					 * same as disconnection, and retry from archive/pg_wal
3684					 * again. The WAL in the archive should be identical to
3685					 * what was streamed, so it's unlikely that it helps, but
3686					 * one can hope...
3687					 */
3688
3689					/*
3690					 * We should be able to move to XLOG_FROM_STREAM only in
3691					 * standby mode.
3692					 */
3693					Assert(StandbyMode);
3694
3695					/*
3696					 * Before we leave XLOG_FROM_STREAM state, make sure that
3697					 * walreceiver is not active, so that it won't overwrite
3698					 * WAL that we restore from archive.
3699					 *
3700					 * If walreceiver is actively streaming (or attempting to
3701					 * connect), we must shut it down. However, if it's
3702					 * already in WAITING state (e.g., due to timeline
3703					 * divergence), we only need to reset the install flag to
3704					 * allow archive restoration.
3705					 */
3706					if (WalRcvStreaming())
3707						XLogShutdownWalRcv();
3708					else
3709					{
3710						ResetInstallXLogFileSegmentActive();
3711					}
3712
3713					/*
3714					 * Before we sleep, re-scan for possible new timelines if
3715					 * we were requested to recover to the latest timeline.
3716					 */
3717					if (recoveryTargetTimeLineGoal == RECOVERY_TARGET_TIMELINE_LATEST)
3718					{
3719						if (rescanLatestTimeLine(replayTLI, replayLSN))
3720						{
3721							currentSource = XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE;
3722							break;
3723						}
3724					}
3725
3726					/*
3727					 * XLOG_FROM_STREAM is the last state in our state
3728					 * machine, so we've exhausted all the options for
3729					 * obtaining the requested WAL. We're going to loop back
3730					 * and retry from the archive, but if it hasn't been long
3731					 * since last attempt, sleep wal_retrieve_retry_interval
3732					 * milliseconds to avoid busy-waiting.
3733					 */
3734					now = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3735					if (!TimestampDifferenceExceeds(last_fail_time, now,
3736													wal_retrieve_retry_interval))
3737					{
3738						long		wait_time;
3739
3740						wait_time = wal_retrieve_retry_interval -
3741							TimestampDifferenceMilliseconds(last_fail_time, now);
3742
3743						elog(LOG, "waiting for WAL to become available at %X/%X",
3744							 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(RecPtr));
3745
3746						/* Do background tasks that might benefit us later. */
3747						KnownAssignedTransactionIdsIdleMaintenance();
3748
3749						(void) WaitLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch,
3750										 WL_LATCH_SET | WL_TIMEOUT |
3751										 WL_EXIT_ON_PM_DEATH,
3752										 wait_time,
3753										 WAIT_EVENT_RECOVERY_RETRIEVE_RETRY_INTERVAL);
3754						ResetLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch);
3755						now = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3756
3757						/* Handle interrupt signals of startup process */
3758						ProcessStartupProcInterrupts();
3759					}
3760					last_fail_time = now;
3761					currentSource = XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE;
3762					break;
3763
3764				default:
3765					elog(ERROR, "unexpected WAL source %d", currentSource);
3766			}
3767		}
3768		else if (currentSource == XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL)
3769		{
3770			/*
3771			 * We just successfully read a file in pg_wal. We prefer files in
3772			 * the archive over ones in pg_wal, so try the next file again
3773			 * from the archive first.
3774			 */
3775			if (InArchiveRecovery)
3776				currentSource = XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE;
3777		}
3778
3779		if (currentSource != oldSource)
3780			elog(DEBUG2, "switched WAL source from %s to %s after %s",
3781				 xlogSourceNames[oldSource], xlogSourceNames[currentSource],
3782				 lastSourceFailed ? "failure" : "success");
3783
3784		/*
3785		 * We've now handled possible failure. Try to read from the chosen
3786		 * source.
3787		 */
3788		lastSourceFailed = false;
3789
3790		switch (currentSource)
3791		{
3792			case XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE:
3793			case XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL:
3794
3795				/*
3796				 * WAL receiver must not be running when reading WAL from
3797				 * archive or pg_wal.
3798				 */
3799				Assert(!WalRcvStreaming());
3800
3801				/* Close any old file we might have open. */
3802				if (readFile >= 0)
3803				{
3804					close(readFile);
3805					readFile = -1;
3806				}
3807				/* Reset curFileTLI if random fetch. */
3808				if (randAccess)
3809					curFileTLI = 0;
3810
3811				/*
3812				 * Try to restore the file from archive, or read an existing
3813				 * file from pg_wal.
3814				 */
3815				readFile = XLogFileReadAnyTLI(readSegNo,
3816											  currentSource == XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE ? XLOG_FROM_ANY :
3817											  currentSource);
3818				if (readFile >= 0)
3819					return XLREAD_SUCCESS;	/* success! */
3820
3821				/*
3822				 * Nope, not found in archive or pg_wal.
3823				 */
3824				lastSourceFailed = true;
3825				break;
3826
3827			case XLOG_FROM_STREAM:
3828				{
3829					bool		havedata;
3830
3831					/*
3832					 * We should be able to move to XLOG_FROM_STREAM only in
3833					 * standby mode.
3834					 */
3835					Assert(StandbyMode);
3836
3837					/*
3838					 * First, shutdown walreceiver if its restart has been
3839					 * requested -- but no point if we're already slated for
3840					 * starting it.
3841					 */
3842					if (pendingWalRcvRestart && !startWalReceiver)
3843					{
3844						XLogShutdownWalRcv();
3845
3846						/*
3847						 * Re-scan for possible new timelines if we were
3848						 * requested to recover to the latest timeline.
3849						 */
3850						if (recoveryTargetTimeLineGoal ==
3851							RECOVERY_TARGET_TIMELINE_LATEST)
3852							rescanLatestTimeLine(replayTLI, replayLSN);
3853
3854						startWalReceiver = true;
3855					}
3856					pendingWalRcvRestart = false;
3857
3858					/*
3859					 * Launch walreceiver if needed.
3860					 *
3861					 * If fetching_ckpt is true, RecPtr points to the initial
3862					 * checkpoint location. In that case, we use RedoStartLSN
3863					 * as the streaming start position instead of RecPtr, so
3864					 * that when we later jump backwards to start redo at
3865					 * RedoStartLSN, we will have the logs streamed already.
3866					 */
3867					if (startWalReceiver &&
3868						PrimaryConnInfo && strcmp(PrimaryConnInfo, "") != 0)
3869					{
3870						XLogRecPtr	ptr;
3871						TimeLineID	tli;
3872
3873						if (fetching_ckpt)
3874						{
3875							ptr = RedoStartLSN;
3876							tli = RedoStartTLI;
3877						}
3878						else
3879						{
3880							ptr = RecPtr;
3881
3882							/*
3883							 * Use the record begin position to determine the
3884							 * TLI, rather than the position we're reading.
3885							 */
3886							tli = tliOfPointInHistory(tliRecPtr, expectedTLEs);
3887
3888							if (curFileTLI > 0 && tli < curFileTLI)
3889								elog(ERROR, "according to history file, WAL location %X/%X belongs to timeline %u, but previous recovered WAL file came from timeline %u",
3890									 LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(tliRecPtr),
3891									 tli, curFileTLI);
3892						}
3893						curFileTLI = tli;
3894						SetInstallXLogFileSegmentActive();
3895						RequestXLogStreaming(tli, ptr, PrimaryConnInfo,
3896											 PrimarySlotName,
3897											 wal_receiver_create_temp_slot);
3898						flushedUpto = 0;
3899					}
3900
3901					/*
3902					 * Check if WAL receiver is active or wait to start up.
3903					 */
3904					if (!WalRcvStreaming())
3905					{
3906						lastSourceFailed = true;
3907						break;
3908					}
3909
3910					/*
3911					 * Walreceiver is active, so see if new data has arrived.
3912					 *
3913					 * We only advance XLogReceiptTime when we obtain fresh
3914					 * WAL from walreceiver and observe that we had already
3915					 * processed everything before the most recent "chunk"
3916					 * that it flushed to disk.  In steady state where we are
3917					 * keeping up with the incoming data, XLogReceiptTime will
3918					 * be updated on each cycle. When we are behind,
3919					 * XLogReceiptTime will not advance, so the grace time
3920					 * allotted to conflicting queries will decrease.
3921					 */
3922					if (RecPtr < flushedUpto)
3923						havedata = true;
3924					else
3925					{
3926						XLogRecPtr	latestChunkStart;
3927
3928						flushedUpto = GetWalRcvFlushRecPtr(&latestChunkStart, &receiveTLI);
3929						if (RecPtr < flushedUpto && receiveTLI == curFileTLI)
3930						{
3931							havedata = true;
3932							if (latestChunkStart <= RecPtr)
3933							{
3934								XLogReceiptTime = GetCurrentTimestamp();
3935								SetCurrentChunkStartTime(XLogReceiptTime);
3936							}
3937						}
3938						else
3939							havedata = false;
3940					}
3941					if (havedata)
3942					{
3943						/*
3944						 * Great, streamed far enough.  Open the file if it's
3945						 * not open already.  Also read the timeline history
3946						 * file if we haven't initialized timeline history
3947						 * yet; it should be streamed over and present in
3948						 * pg_wal by now.  Use XLOG_FROM_STREAM so that source
3949						 * info is set correctly and XLogReceiptTime isn't
3950						 * changed.
3951						 *
3952						 * NB: We must set readTimeLineHistory based on
3953						 * recoveryTargetTLI, not receiveTLI. Normally they'll
3954						 * be the same, but if recovery_target_timeline is
3955						 * 'latest' and archiving is configured, then it's
3956						 * possible that we managed to retrieve one or more
3957						 * new timeline history files from the archive,
3958						 * updating recoveryTargetTLI.
3959						 */
3960						if (readFile < 0)
3961						{
3962							if (!expectedTLEs)
3963								expectedTLEs = readTimeLineHistory(recoveryTargetTLI);
3964							readFile = XLogFileRead(readSegNo, receiveTLI,
3965													XLOG_FROM_STREAM, false);
3966							Assert(readFile >= 0);
3967						}
3968						else
3969						{
3970							/* just make sure source info is correct... */
3971							readSource = XLOG_FROM_STREAM;
3972							XLogReceiptSource = XLOG_FROM_STREAM;
3973							return XLREAD_SUCCESS;
3974						}
3975						break;
3976					}
3977
3978					/* In nonblocking mode, return rather than sleeping. */
3979					if (nonblocking)
3980						return XLREAD_WOULDBLOCK;
3981
3982					/*
3983					 * Data not here yet. Check for trigger, then wait for
3984					 * walreceiver to wake us up when new WAL arrives.
3985					 */
3986					if (CheckForStandbyTrigger())
3987					{
3988						/*
3989						 * Note that we don't return XLREAD_FAIL immediately
3990						 * here. After being triggered, we still want to
3991						 * replay all the WAL that was already streamed. It's
3992						 * in pg_wal now, so we just treat this as a failure,
3993						 * and the state machine will move on to replay the
3994						 * streamed WAL from pg_wal, and then recheck the
3995						 * trigger and exit replay.
3996						 */
3997						lastSourceFailed = true;
3998						break;
3999					}
4000
4001					/*
4002					 * Since we have replayed everything we have received so
4003					 * far and are about to start waiting for more WAL, let's
4004					 * tell the upstream server our replay location now so
4005					 * that pg_stat_replication doesn't show stale
4006					 * information.
4007					 */
4008					if (!streaming_reply_sent)
4009					{
4010						WalRcvForceReply();
4011						streaming_reply_sent = true;
4012					}
4013
4014					/* Do any background tasks that might benefit us later. */
4015					KnownAssignedTransactionIdsIdleMaintenance();
4016
4017					/* Update pg_stat_recovery_prefetch before sleeping. */
4018					XLogPrefetcherComputeStats(xlogprefetcher);
4019
4020					/*
4021					 * Wait for more WAL to arrive, when we will be woken
4022					 * immediately by the WAL receiver.
4023					 */
4024					(void) WaitLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch,
4025									 WL_LATCH_SET | WL_EXIT_ON_PM_DEATH,
4026									 -1L,
4027									 WAIT_EVENT_RECOVERY_WAL_STREAM);
4028					ResetLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch);
4029					break;
4030				}
4031
4032			default:
4033				elog(ERROR, "unexpected WAL source %d", currentSource);
4034		}
4035
4036		/*
4037		 * Check for recovery pause here so that we can confirm more quickly
4038		 * that a requested pause has actually taken effect.
4039		 */
4040		if (((volatile XLogRecoveryCtlData *) XLogRecoveryCtl)->recoveryPauseState !=
4041			RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED)
4042			recoveryPausesHere(false);
4043
4044		/*
4045		 * This possibly-long loop needs to handle interrupts of startup
4046		 * process.
4047		 */
4048		ProcessStartupProcInterrupts();
4049	}
4050
4051	return XLREAD_FAIL;			/* not reached */
4052}
4053
4054
4055/*
4056 * Determine what log level should be used to report a corrupt WAL record
4057 * in the current WAL page, previously read by XLogPageRead().
4058 *
4059 * 'emode' is the error mode that would be used to report a file-not-found
4060 * or legitimate end-of-WAL situation.   Generally, we use it as-is, but if
4061 * we're retrying the exact same record that we've tried previously, only
4062 * complain the first time to keep the noise down.  However, we only do when
4063 * reading from pg_wal, because we don't expect any invalid records in archive
4064 * or in records streamed from the primary. Files in the archive should be complete,
4065 * and we should never hit the end of WAL because we stop and wait for more WAL
4066 * to arrive before replaying it.
4067 *
4068 * NOTE: This function remembers the RecPtr value it was last called with,
4069 * to suppress repeated messages about the same record. Only call this when
4070 * you are about to ereport(), or you might cause a later message to be
4071 * erroneously suppressed.
4072 */
4073static int
4074emode_for_corrupt_record(int emode, XLogRecPtr RecPtr)
4075{
4076	static XLogRecPtr lastComplaint = 0;
4077
4078	if (readSource == XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL && emode == LOG)
4079	{
4080		if (RecPtr == lastComplaint)
4081			emode = DEBUG1;
4082		else
4083			lastComplaint = RecPtr;
4084	}
4085	return emode;
4086}
4087
4088
4089/*
4090 * Subroutine to try to fetch and validate a prior checkpoint record.
4091 */
4092static XLogRecord *
4093ReadCheckpointRecord(XLogPrefetcher *xlogprefetcher, XLogRecPtr RecPtr,
4094					 TimeLineID replayTLI)
4095{
4096	XLogRecord *record;
4097	uint8		info;
4098
4099	Assert(xlogreader != NULL);
4100
4101	if (!XRecOffIsValid(RecPtr))
4102	{
4103		ereport(LOG,
4104				(errmsg("invalid checkpoint location")));
4105		return NULL;
4106	}
4107
4108	XLogPrefetcherBeginRead(xlogprefetcher, RecPtr);
4109	record = ReadRecord(xlogprefetcher, LOG, true, replayTLI);
4110
4111	if (record == NULL)
4112	{
4113		ereport(LOG,
4114				(errmsg("invalid checkpoint record")));
4115		return NULL;
4116	}
4117	if (record->xl_rmid != RM_XLOG_ID)
4118	{
4119		ereport(LOG,
4120				(errmsg("invalid resource manager ID in checkpoint record")));
4121		return NULL;
4122	}
4123	info = record->xl_info & ~XLR_INFO_MASK;
4124	if (info != XLOG_CHECKPOINT_SHUTDOWN &&
4125		info != XLOG_CHECKPOINT_ONLINE)
4126	{
4127		ereport(LOG,
4128				(errmsg("invalid xl_info in checkpoint record")));
4129		return NULL;
4130	}
4131	if (record->xl_tot_len != SizeOfXLogRecord + SizeOfXLogRecordDataHeaderShort + sizeof(CheckPoint))
4132	{
4133		ereport(LOG,
4134				(errmsg("invalid length of checkpoint record")));
4135		return NULL;
4136	}
4137	return record;
4138}
4139
4140/*
4141 * Scan for new timelines that might have appeared in the archive since we
4142 * started recovery.
4143 *
4144 * If there are any, the function changes recovery target TLI to the latest
4145 * one and returns 'true'.
4146 */
4147static bool
4148rescanLatestTimeLine(TimeLineID replayTLI, XLogRecPtr replayLSN)
4149{
4150	List	   *newExpectedTLEs;
4151	bool		found;
4152	ListCell   *cell;
4153	TimeLineID	newtarget;
4154	TimeLineID	oldtarget = recoveryTargetTLI;
4155	TimeLineHistoryEntry *currentTle = NULL;
4156
4157	newtarget = findNewestTimeLine(recoveryTargetTLI);
4158	if (newtarget == recoveryTargetTLI)
4159	{
4160		/* No new timelines found */
4161		return false;
4162	}
4163
4164	/*
4165	 * Determine the list of expected TLIs for the new TLI
4166	 */
4167
4168	newExpectedTLEs = readTimeLineHistory(newtarget);
4169
4170	/*
4171	 * If the current timeline is not part of the history of the new timeline,
4172	 * we cannot proceed to it.
4173	 */
4174	found = false;
4175	foreach(cell, newExpectedTLEs)
4176	{
4177		currentTle = (TimeLineHistoryEntry *) lfirst(cell);
4178
4179		if (currentTle->tli == recoveryTargetTLI)
4180		{
4181			found = true;
4182			break;
4183		}
4184	}
4185	if (!found)
4186	{
4187		ereport(LOG,
4188				(errmsg("new timeline %u is not a child of database system timeline %u",
4189						newtarget,
4190						replayTLI)));
4191		return false;
4192	}
4193
4194	/*
4195	 * The current timeline was found in the history file, but check that the
4196	 * next timeline was forked off from it *after* the current recovery
4197	 * location.
4198	 */
4199	if (currentTle->end < replayLSN)
4200	{
4201		ereport(LOG,
4202				(errmsg("new timeline %u forked off current database system timeline %u before current recovery point %X/%X",
4203						newtarget,
4204						replayTLI,
4205						LSN_FORMAT_ARGS(replayLSN))));
4206		return false;
4207	}
4208
4209	/* The new timeline history seems valid. Switch target */
4210	recoveryTargetTLI = newtarget;
4211	list_free_deep(expectedTLEs);
4212	expectedTLEs = newExpectedTLEs;
4213
4214	/*
4215	 * As in StartupXLOG(), try to ensure we have all the history files
4216	 * between the old target and new target in pg_wal.
4217	 */
4218	restoreTimeLineHistoryFiles(oldtarget + 1, newtarget);
4219
4220	ereport(LOG,
4221			(errmsg("new target timeline is %u",
4222					recoveryTargetTLI)));
4223
4224	return true;
4225}
4226
4227
4228/*
4229 * Open a logfile segment for reading (during recovery).
4230 *
4231 * If source == XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE, the segment is retrieved from archive.
4232 * Otherwise, it's assumed to be already available in pg_wal.
4233 */
4234static int
4235XLogFileRead(XLogSegNo segno, TimeLineID tli,
4236			 XLogSource source, bool notfoundOk)
4237{
4238	char		xlogfname[MAXFNAMELEN];
4239	char		activitymsg[MAXFNAMELEN + 16];
4240	char		path[MAXPGPATH];
4241	int			fd;
4242
4243	XLogFileName(xlogfname, tli, segno, wal_segment_size);
4244
4245	switch (source)
4246	{
4247		case XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE:
4248			/* Report recovery progress in PS display */
4249			snprintf(activitymsg, sizeof(activitymsg), "waiting for %s",
4250					 xlogfname);
4251			set_ps_display(activitymsg);
4252
4253			if (!RestoreArchivedFile(path, xlogfname,
4254									 "RECOVERYXLOG",
4255									 wal_segment_size,
4256									 InRedo))
4257				return -1;
4258			break;
4259
4260		case XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL:
4261		case XLOG_FROM_STREAM:
4262			XLogFilePath(path, tli, segno, wal_segment_size);
4263			break;
4264
4265		default:
4266			elog(ERROR, "invalid XLogFileRead source %d", source);
4267	}
4268
4269	/*
4270	 * If the segment was fetched from archival storage, replace the existing
4271	 * xlog segment (if any) with the archival version.
4272	 */
4273	if (source == XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE)
4274	{
4275		Assert(!IsInstallXLogFileSegmentActive());
4276		KeepFileRestoredFromArchive(path, xlogfname);
4277
4278		/*
4279		 * Set path to point at the new file in pg_wal.
4280		 */
4281		snprintf(path, MAXPGPATH, XLOGDIR "/%s", xlogfname);
4282	}
4283
4284	fd = BasicOpenFile(path, O_RDONLY | PG_BINARY);
4285	if (fd >= 0)
4286	{
4287		/* Success! */
4288		curFileTLI = tli;
4289
4290		/* Report recovery progress in PS display */
4291		snprintf(activitymsg, sizeof(activitymsg), "recovering %s",
4292				 xlogfname);
4293		set_ps_display(activitymsg);
4294
4295		/* Track source of data in assorted state variables */
4296		readSource = source;
4297		XLogReceiptSource = source;
4298		/* In FROM_STREAM case, caller tracks receipt time, not me */
4299		if (source != XLOG_FROM_STREAM)
4300			XLogReceiptTime = GetCurrentTimestamp();
4301
4302		return fd;
4303	}
4304	if (errno != ENOENT || !notfoundOk) /* unexpected failure? */
4305		ereport(PANIC,
4306				(errcode_for_file_access(),
4307				 errmsg("could not open file \"%s\": %m", path)));
4308	return -1;
4309}
4310
4311/*
4312 * Open a logfile segment for reading (during recovery).
4313 *
4314 * This version searches for the segment with any TLI listed in expectedTLEs.
4315 */
4316static int
4317XLogFileReadAnyTLI(XLogSegNo segno, XLogSource source)
4318{
4319	char		path[MAXPGPATH];
4320	ListCell   *cell;
4321	int			fd;
4322	List	   *tles;
4323
4324	/*
4325	 * Loop looking for a suitable timeline ID: we might need to read any of
4326	 * the timelines listed in expectedTLEs.
4327	 *
4328	 * We expect curFileTLI on entry to be the TLI of the preceding file in
4329	 * sequence, or 0 if there was no predecessor.  We do not allow curFileTLI
4330	 * to go backwards; this prevents us from picking up the wrong file when a
4331	 * parent timeline extends to higher segment numbers than the child we
4332	 * want to read.
4333	 *
4334	 * If we haven't read the timeline history file yet, read it now, so that
4335	 * we know which TLIs to scan.  We don't save the list in expectedTLEs,
4336	 * however, unless we actually find a valid segment.  That way if there is
4337	 * neither a timeline history file nor a WAL segment in the archive, and
4338	 * streaming replication is set up, we'll read the timeline history file
4339	 * streamed from the primary when we start streaming, instead of
4340	 * recovering with a dummy history generated here.
4341	 */
4342	if (expectedTLEs)
4343		tles = expectedTLEs;
4344	else
4345		tles = readTimeLineHistory(recoveryTargetTLI);
4346
4347	foreach(cell, tles)
4348	{
4349		TimeLineHistoryEntry *hent = (TimeLineHistoryEntry *) lfirst(cell);
4350		TimeLineID	tli = hent->tli;
4351
4352		if (tli < curFileTLI)
4353			break;				/* don't bother looking at too-old TLIs */
4354
4355		/*
4356		 * Skip scanning the timeline ID that the logfile segment to read
4357		 * doesn't belong to
4358		 */
4359		if (hent->begin != InvalidXLogRecPtr)
4360		{
4361			XLogSegNo	beginseg = 0;
4362
4363			XLByteToSeg(hent->begin, beginseg, wal_segment_size);
4364
4365			/*
4366			 * The logfile segment that doesn't belong to the timeline is
4367			 * older or newer than the segment that the timeline started or
4368			 * ended at, respectively. It's sufficient to check only the
4369			 * starting segment of the timeline here. Since the timelines are
4370			 * scanned in descending order in this loop, any segments newer
4371			 * than the ending segment should belong to newer timeline and
4372			 * have already been read before. So it's not necessary to check
4373			 * the ending segment of the timeline here.
4374			 */
4375			if (segno < beginseg)
4376				continue;
4377		}
4378
4379		if (source == XLOG_FROM_ANY || source == XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE)
4380		{
4381			fd = XLogFileRead(segno, tli, XLOG_FROM_ARCHIVE, true);
4382			if (fd != -1)
4383			{
4384				elog(DEBUG1, "got WAL segment from archive");
4385				if (!expectedTLEs)
4386					expectedTLEs = tles;
4387				return fd;
4388			}
4389		}
4390
4391		if (source == XLOG_FROM_ANY || source == XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL)
4392		{
4393			fd = XLogFileRead(segno, tli, XLOG_FROM_PG_WAL, true);
4394			if (fd != -1)
4395			{
4396				if (!expectedTLEs)
4397					expectedTLEs = tles;
4398				return fd;
4399			}
4400		}
4401	}
4402
4403	/* Couldn't find it.  For simplicity, complain about front timeline */
4404	XLogFilePath(path, recoveryTargetTLI, segno, wal_segment_size);
4405	errno = ENOENT;
4406	ereport(DEBUG2,
4407			(errcode_for_file_access(),
4408			 errmsg("could not open file \"%s\": %m", path)));
4409	return -1;
4410}
4411
4412/*
4413 * Set flag to signal the walreceiver to restart.  (The startup process calls
4414 * this on noticing a relevant configuration change.)
4415 */
4416void
4417StartupRequestWalReceiverRestart(void)
4418{
4419	if (currentSource == XLOG_FROM_STREAM && WalRcvRunning())
4420	{
4421		ereport(LOG,
4422				(errmsg("WAL receiver process shutdown requested")));
4423
4424		pendingWalRcvRestart = true;
4425	}
4426}
4427
4428
4429/*
4430 * Has a standby promotion already been triggered?
4431 *
4432 * Unlike CheckForStandbyTrigger(), this works in any process
4433 * that's connected to shared memory.
4434 */
4435bool
4436PromoteIsTriggered(void)
4437{
4438	/*
4439	 * We check shared state each time only until a standby promotion is
4440	 * triggered. We can't trigger a promotion again, so there's no need to
4441	 * keep checking after the shared variable has once been seen true.
4442	 */
4443	if (LocalPromoteIsTriggered)
4444		return true;
4445
4446	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4447	LocalPromoteIsTriggered = XLogRecoveryCtl->SharedPromoteIsTriggered;
4448	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4449
4450	return LocalPromoteIsTriggered;
4451}
4452
4453static void
4454SetPromoteIsTriggered(void)
4455{
4456	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4457	XLogRecoveryCtl->SharedPromoteIsTriggered = true;
4458	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4459
4460	/*
4461	 * Mark the recovery pause state as 'not paused' because the paused state
4462	 * ends and promotion continues if a promotion is triggered while recovery
4463	 * is paused. Otherwise pg_get_wal_replay_pause_state() can mistakenly
4464	 * return 'paused' while a promotion is ongoing.
4465	 */
4466	SetRecoveryPause(false);
4467
4468	LocalPromoteIsTriggered = true;
4469}
4470
4471/*
4472 * Check whether a promote request has arrived.
4473 */
4474static bool
4475CheckForStandbyTrigger(void)
4476{
4477	if (LocalPromoteIsTriggered)
4478		return true;
4479
4480	if (IsPromoteSignaled() && CheckPromoteSignal())
4481	{
4482		ereport(LOG, (errmsg("received promote request")));
4483		RemovePromoteSignalFiles();
4484		ResetPromoteSignaled();
4485		SetPromoteIsTriggered();
4486		return true;
4487	}
4488
4489	return false;
4490}
4491
4492/*
4493 * Remove the files signaling a standby promotion request.
4494 */
4495void
4496RemovePromoteSignalFiles(void)
4497{
4498	unlink(PROMOTE_SIGNAL_FILE);
4499}
4500
4501/*
4502 * Check to see if a promote request has arrived.
4503 */
4504bool
4505CheckPromoteSignal(void)
4506{
4507	struct stat stat_buf;
4508
4509	if (stat(PROMOTE_SIGNAL_FILE, &stat_buf) == 0)
4510		return true;
4511
4512	return false;
4513}
4514
4515/*
4516 * Wake up startup process to replay newly arrived WAL, or to notice that
4517 * failover has been requested.
4518 */
4519void
4520WakeupRecovery(void)
4521{
4522	SetLatch(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryWakeupLatch);
4523}
4524
4525/*
4526 * Schedule a walreceiver wakeup in the main recovery loop.
4527 */
4528void
4529XLogRequestWalReceiverReply(void)
4530{
4531	doRequestWalReceiverReply = true;
4532}
4533
4534/*
4535 * Is HotStandby active yet? This is only important in special backends
4536 * since normal backends won't ever be able to connect until this returns
4537 * true. Postmaster knows this by way of signal, not via shared memory.
4538 *
4539 * Unlike testing standbyState, this works in any process that's connected to
4540 * shared memory.  (And note that standbyState alone doesn't tell the truth
4541 * anyway.)
4542 */
4543bool
4544HotStandbyActive(void)
4545{
4546	/*
4547	 * We check shared state each time only until Hot Standby is active. We
4548	 * can't de-activate Hot Standby, so there's no need to keep checking
4549	 * after the shared variable has once been seen true.
4550	 */
4551	if (LocalHotStandbyActive)
4552		return true;
4553	else
4554	{
4555		/* spinlock is essential on machines with weak memory ordering! */
4556		SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4557		LocalHotStandbyActive = XLogRecoveryCtl->SharedHotStandbyActive;
4558		SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4559
4560		return LocalHotStandbyActive;
4561	}
4562}
4563
4564/*
4565 * Like HotStandbyActive(), but to be used only in WAL replay code,
4566 * where we don't need to ask any other process what the state is.
4567 */
4568static bool
4569HotStandbyActiveInReplay(void)
4570{
4571	Assert(AmStartupProcess() || !IsPostmasterEnvironment);
4572	return LocalHotStandbyActive;
4573}
4574
4575/*
4576 * Get latest redo apply position.
4577 *
4578 * Exported to allow WALReceiver to read the pointer directly.
4579 */
4580XLogRecPtr
4581GetXLogReplayRecPtr(TimeLineID *replayTLI)
4582{
4583	XLogRecPtr	recptr;
4584	TimeLineID	tli;
4585
4586	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4587	recptr = XLogRecoveryCtl->lastReplayedEndRecPtr;
4588	tli = XLogRecoveryCtl->lastReplayedTLI;
4589	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4590
4591	if (replayTLI)
4592		*replayTLI = tli;
4593	return recptr;
4594}
4595
4596
4597/*
4598 * Get position of last applied, or the record being applied.
4599 *
4600 * This is different from GetXLogReplayRecPtr() in that if a WAL
4601 * record is currently being applied, this includes that record.
4602 */
4603XLogRecPtr
4604GetCurrentReplayRecPtr(TimeLineID *replayEndTLI)
4605{
4606	XLogRecPtr	recptr;
4607	TimeLineID	tli;
4608
4609	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4610	recptr = XLogRecoveryCtl->replayEndRecPtr;
4611	tli = XLogRecoveryCtl->replayEndTLI;
4612	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4613
4614	if (replayEndTLI)
4615		*replayEndTLI = tli;
4616	return recptr;
4617}
4618
4619/*
4620 * Save timestamp of latest processed commit/abort record.
4621 *
4622 * We keep this in XLogRecoveryCtl, not a simple static variable, so that it can be
4623 * seen by processes other than the startup process.  Note in particular
4624 * that CreateRestartPoint is executed in the checkpointer.
4625 */
4626static void
4627SetLatestXTime(TimestampTz xtime)
4628{
4629	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4630	XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryLastXTime = xtime;
4631	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4632}
4633
4634/*
4635 * Fetch timestamp of latest processed commit/abort record.
