4636 */
4637TimestampTz
4638GetLatestXTime(void)
4639{
4640	TimestampTz xtime;
4641
4642	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4643	xtime = XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryLastXTime;
4644	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4645
4646	return xtime;
4647}
4648
4649/*
4650 * Save timestamp of the next chunk of WAL records to apply.
4651 *
4652 * We keep this in XLogRecoveryCtl, not a simple static variable, so that it can be
4653 * seen by all backends.
4654 */
4655static void
4656SetCurrentChunkStartTime(TimestampTz xtime)
4657{
4658	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4659	XLogRecoveryCtl->currentChunkStartTime = xtime;
4660	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4661}
4662
4663/*
4664 * Fetch timestamp of latest processed commit/abort record.
4665 * Startup process maintains an accurate local copy in XLogReceiptTime
4666 */
4667TimestampTz
4668GetCurrentChunkReplayStartTime(void)
4669{
4670	TimestampTz xtime;
4671
4672	SpinLockAcquire(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4673	xtime = XLogRecoveryCtl->currentChunkStartTime;
4674	SpinLockRelease(&XLogRecoveryCtl->info_lck);
4675
4676	return xtime;
4677}
4678
4679/*
4680 * Returns time of receipt of current chunk of XLOG data, as well as
4681 * whether it was received from streaming replication or from archives.
4682 */
4683void
4684GetXLogReceiptTime(TimestampTz *rtime, bool *fromStream)
4685{
4686	/*
4687	 * This must be executed in the startup process, since we don't export the
4688	 * relevant state to shared memory.
4689	 */
4690	Assert(InRecovery);
4691
4692	*rtime = XLogReceiptTime;
4693	*fromStream = (XLogReceiptSource == XLOG_FROM_STREAM);
4694}
4695
4696/*
4697 * Note that text field supplied is a parameter name and does not require
4698 * translation
4699 */
4700void
4701RecoveryRequiresIntParameter(const char *param_name, int currValue, int minValue)
4702{
4703	if (currValue < minValue)
4704	{
4705		if (HotStandbyActiveInReplay())
4706		{
4707			bool		warned_for_promote = false;
4708
4709			ereport(WARNING,
4710					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4711					 errmsg("hot standby is not possible because of insufficient parameter settings"),
4712					 errdetail("%s = %d is a lower setting than on the primary server, where its value was %d.",
4713							   param_name,
4714							   currValue,
4715							   minValue)));
4716
4717			SetRecoveryPause(true);
4718
4719			ereport(LOG,
4720					(errmsg("recovery has paused"),
4721					 errdetail("If recovery is unpaused, the server will shut down."),
4722					 errhint("You can then restart the server after making the necessary configuration changes.")));
4723
4724			while (GetRecoveryPauseState() != RECOVERY_NOT_PAUSED)
4725			{
4726				ProcessStartupProcInterrupts();
4727
4728				if (CheckForStandbyTrigger())
4729				{
4730					if (!warned_for_promote)
4731						ereport(WARNING,
4732								(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4733								 errmsg("promotion is not possible because of insufficient parameter settings"),
4734
4735						/*
4736						 * Repeat the detail from above so it's easy to find
4737						 * in the log.
4738						 */
4739								 errdetail("%s = %d is a lower setting than on the primary server, where its value was %d.",
4740										   param_name,
4741										   currValue,
4742										   minValue),
4743								 errhint("Restart the server after making the necessary configuration changes.")));
4744					warned_for_promote = true;
4745				}
4746
4747				/*
4748				 * If recovery pause is requested then set it paused.  While
4749				 * we are in the loop, user might resume and pause again so
4750				 * set this every time.
4751				 */
4752				ConfirmRecoveryPaused();
4753
4754				/*
4755				 * We wait on a condition variable that will wake us as soon
4756				 * as the pause ends, but we use a timeout so we can check the
4757				 * above conditions periodically too.
4758				 */
4759				ConditionVariableTimedSleep(&XLogRecoveryCtl->recoveryNotPausedCV, 1000,
4760											WAIT_EVENT_RECOVERY_PAUSE);
4761			}
4762			ConditionVariableCancelSleep();
4763		}
4764
4765		ereport(FATAL,
4766				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4767				 errmsg("recovery aborted because of insufficient parameter settings"),
4768		/* Repeat the detail from above so it's easy to find in the log. */
4769				 errdetail("%s = %d is a lower setting than on the primary server, where its value was %d.",
4770						   param_name,
4771						   currValue,
4772						   minValue),
4773				 errhint("You can restart the server after making the necessary configuration changes.")));
4774	}
4775}
4776
4777
4778/*
4779 * GUC check_hook for primary_slot_name
4780 */
4781bool
4782check_primary_slot_name(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4783{
4784	int			err_code;
4785	char	   *err_msg = NULL;
4786	char	   *err_hint = NULL;
4787
4788	if (*newval && strcmp(*newval, "") != 0 &&
4789		!ReplicationSlotValidateNameInternal(*newval, &err_code, &err_msg,
4790											 &err_hint))
4791	{
4792		GUC_check_errcode(err_code);
4793		GUC_check_errdetail("%s", err_msg);
4794		if (err_hint != NULL)
4795			GUC_check_errhint("%s", err_hint);
4796		return false;
4797	}
4798
4799	return true;
4800}
4801
4802/*
4803 * Recovery target settings: Only one of the several recovery_target* settings
4804 * may be set.  Setting a second one results in an error.  The global variable
4805 * recoveryTarget tracks which kind of recovery target was chosen.  Other
4806 * variables store the actual target value (for example a string or a xid).
4807 * The assign functions of the parameters check whether a competing parameter
4808 * was already set.  But we want to allow setting the same parameter multiple
4809 * times.  We also want to allow unsetting a parameter and setting a different
4810 * one, so we unset recoveryTarget when the parameter is set to an empty
4811 * string.
4812 *
4813 * XXX this code is broken by design.  Throwing an error from a GUC assign
4814 * hook breaks fundamental assumptions of guc.c.  So long as all the variables
4815 * for which this can happen are PGC_POSTMASTER, the consequences are limited,
4816 * since we'd just abort postmaster startup anyway.  Nonetheless it's likely
4817 * that we have odd behaviors such as unexpected GUC ordering dependencies.
4818 */
4819
4820pg_noreturn static void
4821error_multiple_recovery_targets(void)
4822{
4823	ereport(ERROR,
4824			(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4825			 errmsg("multiple recovery targets specified"),
4826			 errdetail("At most one of \"recovery_target\", \"recovery_target_lsn\", \"recovery_target_name\", \"recovery_target_time\", \"recovery_target_xid\" may be set.")));
4827}
4828
4829/*
4830 * GUC check_hook for recovery_target
4831 */
4832bool
4833check_recovery_target(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4834{
4835	if (strcmp(*newval, "immediate") != 0 && strcmp(*newval, "") != 0)
4836	{
4837		GUC_check_errdetail("The only allowed value is \"immediate\".");
4838		return false;
4839	}
4840	return true;
4841}
4842
4843/*
4844 * GUC assign_hook for recovery_target
4845 */
4846void
4847assign_recovery_target(const char *newval, void *extra)
4848{
4849	if (recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET &&
4850		recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE)
4851		error_multiple_recovery_targets();
4852
4853	if (newval && strcmp(newval, "") != 0)
4854		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_IMMEDIATE;
4855	else
4856		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET;
4857}
4858
4859/*
4860 * GUC check_hook for recovery_target_lsn
4861 */
4862bool
4863check_recovery_target_lsn(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4864{
4865	if (strcmp(*newval, "") != 0)
4866	{
4867		XLogRecPtr	lsn;
4868		XLogRecPtr *myextra;
4869		bool		have_error = false;
4870
4871		lsn = pg_lsn_in_internal(*newval, &have_error);
4872		if (have_error)
4873			return false;
4874
4875		myextra = (XLogRecPtr *) guc_malloc(LOG, sizeof(XLogRecPtr));
4876		if (!myextra)
4877			return false;
4878		*myextra = lsn;
4879		*extra = myextra;
4880	}
4881	return true;
4882}
4883
4884/*
4885 * GUC assign_hook for recovery_target_lsn
4886 */
4887void
4888assign_recovery_target_lsn(const char *newval, void *extra)
4889{
4890	if (recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET &&
4891		recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_LSN)
4892		error_multiple_recovery_targets();
4893
4894	if (newval && strcmp(newval, "") != 0)
4895	{
4896		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_LSN;
4897		recoveryTargetLSN = *((XLogRecPtr *) extra);
4898	}
4899	else
4900		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET;
4901}
4902
4903/*
4904 * GUC check_hook for recovery_target_name
4905 */
4906bool
4907check_recovery_target_name(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4908{
4909	/* Use the value of newval directly */
4910	if (strlen(*newval) >= MAXFNAMELEN)
4911	{
4912		GUC_check_errdetail("\"%s\" is too long (maximum %d characters).",
4913							"recovery_target_name", MAXFNAMELEN - 1);
4914		return false;
4915	}
4916	return true;
4917}
4918
4919/*
4920 * GUC assign_hook for recovery_target_name
4921 */
4922void
4923assign_recovery_target_name(const char *newval, void *extra)
4924{
4925	if (recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET &&
4926		recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_NAME)
4927		error_multiple_recovery_targets();
4928
4929	if (newval && strcmp(newval, "") != 0)
4930	{
4931		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_NAME;
4932		recoveryTargetName = newval;
4933	}
4934	else
4935		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET;
4936}
4937
4938/*
4939 * GUC check_hook for recovery_target_time
4940 *
4941 * The interpretation of the recovery_target_time string can depend on the
4942 * time zone setting, so we need to wait until after all GUC processing is
4943 * done before we can do the final parsing of the string.  This check function
4944 * only does a parsing pass to catch syntax errors, but we store the string
4945 * and parse it again when we need to use it.
4946 */
4947bool
4948check_recovery_target_time(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
4949{
4950	if (strcmp(*newval, "") != 0)
4951	{
4952		/* reject some special values */
4953		if (strcmp(*newval, "now") == 0 ||
4954			strcmp(*newval, "today") == 0 ||
4955			strcmp(*newval, "tomorrow") == 0 ||
4956			strcmp(*newval, "yesterday") == 0)
4957		{
4958			return false;
4959		}
4960
4961		/*
4962		 * parse timestamp value (see also timestamptz_in())
4963		 */
4964		{
4965			char	   *str = *newval;
4966			fsec_t		fsec;
4967			struct pg_tm tt,
4968					   *tm = &tt;
4969			int			tz;
4970			int			dtype;
4971			int			nf;
4972			int			dterr;
4973			char	   *field[MAXDATEFIELDS];
4974			int			ftype[MAXDATEFIELDS];
4975			char		workbuf[MAXDATELEN + MAXDATEFIELDS];
4976			DateTimeErrorExtra dtextra;
4977			TimestampTz timestamp;
4978
4979			dterr = ParseDateTime(str, workbuf, sizeof(workbuf),
4980								  field, ftype, MAXDATEFIELDS, &nf);
4981			if (dterr == 0)
4982				dterr = DecodeDateTime(field, ftype, nf,
4983									   &dtype, tm, &fsec, &tz, &dtextra);
4984			if (dterr != 0)
4985				return false;
4986			if (dtype != DTK_DATE)
4987				return false;
4988
4989			if (tm2timestamp(tm, fsec, &tz, &timestamp) != 0)
4990			{
4991				GUC_check_errdetail("Timestamp out of range: \"%s\".", str);
4992				return false;
4993			}
4994		}
4995	}
4996	return true;
4997}
4998
4999/*
5000 * GUC assign_hook for recovery_target_time
5001 */
5002void
5003assign_recovery_target_time(const char *newval, void *extra)
5004{
5005	if (recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET &&
5006		recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_TIME)
5007		error_multiple_recovery_targets();
5008
5009	if (newval && strcmp(newval, "") != 0)
5010		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_TIME;
5011	else
5012		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET;
5013}
5014
5015/*
5016 * GUC check_hook for recovery_target_timeline
5017 */
5018bool
5019check_recovery_target_timeline(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
5020{
5021	RecoveryTargetTimeLineGoal rttg;
5022	RecoveryTargetTimeLineGoal *myextra;
5023
5024	if (strcmp(*newval, "current") == 0)
5025		rttg = RECOVERY_TARGET_TIMELINE_CONTROLFILE;
5026	else if (strcmp(*newval, "latest") == 0)
5027		rttg = RECOVERY_TARGET_TIMELINE_LATEST;
5028	else
5029	{
5030		rttg = RECOVERY_TARGET_TIMELINE_NUMERIC;
5031
5032		errno = 0;
5033		strtoul(*newval, NULL, 0);
5034		if (errno == EINVAL || errno == ERANGE)
5035		{
5036			GUC_check_errdetail("\"recovery_target_timeline\" is not a valid number.");
5037			return false;
5038		}
5039	}
5040
5041	myextra = (RecoveryTargetTimeLineGoal *) guc_malloc(LOG, sizeof(RecoveryTargetTimeLineGoal));
5042	if (!myextra)
5043		return false;
5044	*myextra = rttg;
5045	*extra = myextra;
5046
5047	return true;
5048}
5049
5050/*
5051 * GUC assign_hook for recovery_target_timeline
5052 */
5053void
5054assign_recovery_target_timeline(const char *newval, void *extra)
5055{
5056	recoveryTargetTimeLineGoal = *((RecoveryTargetTimeLineGoal *) extra);
5057	if (recoveryTargetTimeLineGoal == RECOVERY_TARGET_TIMELINE_NUMERIC)
5058		recoveryTargetTLIRequested = (TimeLineID) strtoul(newval, NULL, 0);
5059	else
5060		recoveryTargetTLIRequested = 0;
5061}
5062
5063/*
5064 * GUC check_hook for recovery_target_xid
5065 */
5066bool
5067check_recovery_target_xid(char **newval, void **extra, GucSource source)
5068{
5069	if (strcmp(*newval, "") != 0)
5070	{
5071		TransactionId xid;
5072		TransactionId *myextra;
5073
5074		errno = 0;
5075		xid = (TransactionId) strtou64(*newval, NULL, 0);
5076		if (errno == EINVAL || errno == ERANGE)
5077			return false;
5078
5079		myextra = (TransactionId *) guc_malloc(LOG, sizeof(TransactionId));
5080		if (!myextra)
5081			return false;
5082		*myextra = xid;
5083		*extra = myextra;
5084	}
5085	return true;
5086}
5087
5088/*
5089 * GUC assign_hook for recovery_target_xid
5090 */
5091void
5092assign_recovery_target_xid(const char *newval, void *extra)
5093{
5094	if (recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET &&
5095		recoveryTarget != RECOVERY_TARGET_XID)
5096		error_multiple_recovery_targets();
5097
5098	if (newval && strcmp(newval, "") != 0)
5099	{
5100		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_XID;
5101		recoveryTargetXid = *((TransactionId *) extra);
5102	}
5103	else
5104		recoveryTarget = RECOVERY_TARGET_UNSET;
5105}
